verdict: 2026-07-06 12:13:00 PDT — Sound approach. My reference design was a separate CI shell-lint job over `shfmt -f .`, pinned/installable `shfmt`, default ShellCheck strictness with only the intentional `SC2016` literals suppressed, and a narrow per-finding gitleaks suppression rather than whole-file allowlists. The implementation follows that shape: no product code is touched, no existing dependency or package script is being bypassed, the CI logic is small enough for the problem, and the `.gitleaksignore` fingerprints avoid the previous whole-file blind-spot design. I read the full changed text files plus the package manifests and existing hook, checked the binary image metadata, and verified `shfmt -d -i 2 -ci $(shfmt -f .)`, `shellcheck $(shfmt -f .)`, and `gitleaks detect --no-banner` all exit 0 locally.
